steps.title: Identify what the conclusion requires
steps.content: The conclusion is that M. collaris associates T. caesius alarm calls specifically with danger. To support this, evidence must show the birds' reaction is to these alarm calls in particular—not just any loud or unfamiliar sound.

steps.title: Find the control that proves specificity
steps.content: Choice D shows random noise produces no response from M. collaris, proving their freezing/scattering is specific to T. caesius alarm calls, not a generic startle to any sound. This directly supports the danger-association conclusion. The other choices either confound the evidence (A), observe other species (B), or show consistency without proving the danger-meaning (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
